name: Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38) — circular desde Puente Blanca
region_intro: Hiking around Íscar, located in the Spanish province of Valladolid, offers diverse routes through natural landscapes. The region is characterized by pine forests, river valleys, and gentle elevations, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. These trails wind along rivers like the Eresma and Cega, showcasing the natural beauty of Castile and León. Best hiking trails around Íscar The most popular hiking route is Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38) — circular desde Puente Blanca, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route starts from Puente Blanca and explores the local forest areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop, a moderate 10.6 miles (17.1 km) path. This route leads through serene pine forests and alongside the Eresma River, offering a longer exploration of the natural environment. Local hikers also love the Olmedo City Walls Trail, a 1.7 miles (2.8 km) trail leading through the historic town of Olmedo, often completed in about 44 minutes. This easy circular path is suitable for all skill levels. Hiking around Íscar is defined by pine forests, river valleys, and gentle hills. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The routes in Íscar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Íscar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Íscar?
A: Íscar offers a selection of 7 hiking routes, catering to various preferences. These include 1 easy trail, 5 moderate trails, and 1 difficult trail, ensuring options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Íscar?
A: The hiking terrain around Íscar is characterized by diverse natural environments. You can expect to find serene pine forests, river valleys, and gentle hills. Trails often wind along rivers like the Eresma and Cega, offering a pleasant backdrop of the Castilian landscape.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Íscar?
A: Yes, for those seeking an easier walk, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084863275/olmedo-city-walls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmedo City Walls Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 1.7-mile (2.8 km) circular path is suitable for all skill levels and takes you through the historic town of Olmedo, starting from the Mudéjar Theme Park.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes in Íscar?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Íscar are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083817589/senda-de-la-resina-prc-va-38-circular-desde-puente-blanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38)&lt;/a&gt;, which starts from Puente Blanca, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103784200/return-to-monte-san-marugan-prc-va-61-circular-from-megeces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Monte San Marugan (PRC-VA 61)&lt;/a&gt;, which begins in Megeces.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see while hiking in Íscar?
A: The region around Íscar is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore areas with pine forests and river views, and also encounter significant landmarks. Notable attractions include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coca Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3505363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Olmedo&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3711912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mudéjar Tower of San Nicolás&lt;/a&gt; in Íscar itself.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Íscar suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Íscar are suitable for families, especially the easier, shorter circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084863275/olmedo-city-walls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmedo City Walls Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is an easy 1.7-mile path that is ideal for all skill levels and families looking for a pleasant walk.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Íscar?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many natural trails in the Íscar region. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trailheads in Íscar?
A: Parking is typically available near the starting points of many popular trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1083817589/senda-de-la-resina-prc-va-38-circular-desde-puente-blanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Resina (PRC-VA 38)&lt;/a&gt; starts from Puente Blanca, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103784200/return-to-monte-san-marugan-prc-va-61-circular-from-megeces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Monte San Marugan (PRC-VA 61)&lt;/a&gt; from Megeces, both of which are common access points. Specific parking details can often be found on individual route descriptions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Íscar?
A: The region of Íscar, part of Castile and León, offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons provide comfortable weather for exploring the pine forests and river valleys without the intense heat of summer or the chill of winter.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Íscar?
A: Íscar and its surrounding towns like Olmedo and Mojados offer various amenities for visitors. You can find local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options to relax and refuel after your hikes. These towns provide a good base for exploring the region's trails and cultural attractions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Íscar?
A: The hiking routes in Íscar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the serene natural environments, particularly the pine forests and river paths, and the variety of options available for different ability levels.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes around Íscar?
A: Hikes around Íscar vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084863275/olmedo-city-walls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmedo City Walls Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.7-mile route that takes about 44 minutes. Longer, moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103826524/senda-del-ermitano-and-presa-de-alcazaren-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Ermitaño and Presa de Alcazarén Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 10.6 miles, can take around 4 hours and 24 minutes to complete.
tips: 8 km circular path that begins its route in the Recreational Area of Puente Blanca (Íscar). The path goes along the banks of the River Cega and the River Pirón and crosses stone pine (Pinus pinea) and resin pine (Pinus pinaster) forests with the presence of holm oaks (Quercus ilex).
